---
title: "model-setup"
format: html
---

```{r}
if (!require("easypackages")) install.packages("easypackages") #easy package manager

#install and load libraries
#In this practical we need an additional package to run the GWR models, this package is called "GWmodel"
easypackages::packages ("sf", "sp", "spdep", "spatialreg", "GWmodel", "tmap", "mapview", "car", "RColorBrewer", "cowplot", "leafsync", "leaflet.extras2", "mapview", "tidyverse", "readr")
```

## Data wrangling for modelling

```{r}
#clean your environment
rm(list=ls())
london_ward_data <- st_read('data/ward_poly.gpkg')

names(london_ward_data)
```

```{r}
columns_to_use <- c(
  "Subjective.well.being.average.score..2013",
  "Life.Expectancy.2009.13",
  "Childhood.Obesity.2013",
  "Incapacity.Benefit.rate...2013",
  "Unemployment.rate.2013",
  "Crime.rate...2013",
  "Deliberate.Fires...2013",
  "GCSE.point.scores...2013",
  "Unauthorised.Absence.in.All.Schools.......2013",
  "X..dependent.children.in.out.of.work.households...2013",
  "Public.Transport.Accessibility...2013",
  "Homes.with.access.to.open.space...nature..and...greenspace...2013",
  "proportion_brownfield",
  "Rail_Lden_London_55.0.59.9",
  "Rail_Lden_London_60.0.64.9",
  "Rail_Lden_London_65.0.69.9",
  "Rail_Lden_London_70.0.74.9",
  "Rail_Lden_London_..75.0",
  "Road_Lden_London_55.0.59.9",
  "Road_Lden_London_60.0.64.9",
  "Road_Lden_London_65.0.69.9",
  "Road_Lden_London_70.0.74.9",
  "Road_Lden_London_..75.0",
  "annual_ndvi_2013",
  "co2",                                                              
  "nox",                                                             
  "pm10",                                                             
  "pm2.5"
  )

new_column_names <- c(
  "SubWellBeing",
  "LifeExp",
  "ChildObesity",
  "IncBenefitRate",
  "UnempRate",
  "CrimeRate",
  "DelibFires",
  "GCSEScores",
  "UnauthAbsence",
  "DepChildUnempHouseholds",
  "PubTransAccess",
  "HomesOpenAccessNatGreen",
  "BrownfieldProp",
  "RailDen55",
  "RailDen60",
  "RailDen65",
  "RailDen70",
  "RailDen75",
  "RoadDen55",
  "RoadDen60",
  "RoadDen65",
  "RoadDen70",
  "RoadDen75",
  "Ndvi",
  "C02",
  "NOX",
  "PM10",
  "PM25",
  "geom"
)
length(columns_to_use)
length(new_column_names)
```

```{r}
ward_wellbeing <- london_ward_data |> 
  select(all_of(columns_to_use))

names(ward_wellbeing) <- new_column_names
```

## Modelling

### Equations

```{r}
eq_wellb_no_spatial <- SubWellBeing ~ LifeExp + ChildObesity + IncBenefitRate + UnempRate + 
    CrimeRate + DelibFires + GCSEScores + UnauthAbsence + DepChildUnempHouseholds
eq_wellb_std_spatial <- SubWellBeing ~ LifeExp + ChildObesity + IncBenefitRate + UnempRate + 
    CrimeRate + DelibFires + GCSEScores + UnauthAbsence + DepChildUnempHouseholds + 
    PubTransAccess + HomesOpenAccessNatGreen

eq_wellb_all_spatial <- SubWellBeing ~ LifeExp + ChildObesity + IncBenefitRate + UnempRate + 
    CrimeRate + DelibFires + GCSEScores + UnauthAbsence + DepChildUnempHouseholds + 
    PubTransAccess + HomesOpenAccessNatGreen + BrownfieldProp + RailDen55 + RailDen60 + RailDen65 + RailDen70 + RailDen75 + RoadDen55 + RoadDen60 + RoadDen65 + RoadDen70 + RoadDen75 + Ndvi + C02 + NOX + PM10 + PM25

equations <- c(eq_wellb_no_spatial, eq_wellb_std_spatial, eq_wellb_all_spatial)
```

### Error model stuff

```{r}
#creating adjacency matrix, 
ward_wellbeing_nbq <- poly2nb(ward_wellbeing, queen=TRUE) #Queen’s Contiguity neighborhood
ward_wellbeing_nbq_w <- nb2listw(ward_wellbeing_nbq, style="W") #Queen’s neighborhood wights
```

```{r}
#running spatial error model with same weight matrix created previously
spa_errmodel = errorsarlm(eq_wellb_no_spatial,
                          data = ward_wellbeing, 
                          listw = ward_wellbeing_nbq_w, 
                          zero.policy = TRUE)

summary(spa_errmodel, Nagelkerke=T)
```

```{r}
#check residual autocorrelation
mcSEM_global <- moran.mc(spa_errmodel$residuals, 
                         ward_wellbeing_nbq_w, 
                         2999, 
                         alternative="greater")

plot(mcSEM_global)

mcSEM_global
```

```{r}
#add the residual to polygon and plot
ward_wellbeing$res_sem <- residuals(spa_errmodel)
#plot using t-map
semres <- qtm(ward_wellbeing, "res_sem")
 
semres
```

```{r}
sac_model <- sacsarlm(
  formula = eq_wellb_no_spatial,
  data = ward_wellbeing,
  listw = ward_wellbeing_nbq_w
  )
 
summary(sac_model)
```

```{r}
#add the residual to polygon and plot
ward_wellbeing$res_sac <- residuals(sac_model)
 
#plot using t-map
sacres <-qtm(ward_wellbeing, "res_sac")

sacres
```

### GWR stuff

```{r}
#Before we do any analysis for GWR we need to convert our imported sf object into a sp spatial object. As the GWmodel package usually works on sp objects
#converting the sf polygon into sp object
ward_wellbeing_sp <- as_Spatial(ward_wellbeing)
```

```{r}
gwr_fitting <- function(equation, kernel="gaussian") {
  abw <- bw.gwr(equation,
              approach = "AIC", #specified by CV for cross-validation approach or by AIC corrected (AICc), we used AIC 
              adaptive = TRUE,
              kernel = "gaussian", #this can be different function e.g., bisquare,exponential, depend on the prior understanding or choice of the modeler
              data = ward_wellbeing_sp) #give the sp data created earlier

  #print the number of neighbors
  print(paste0("kernel distance:", abw))

  #fitting the model with gwr.basic function
  a.gwr <- gwr.basic(equation, #the equation
                   adaptive = TRUE,
                   kernel = "gaussian", #indicate the Kernel again
                   bw = abw, #give the optimal bandwidth we found in the last stage
                   data = ward_wellbeing_sp)
  
  GWR_AIC <- a.gwr$GW.diagnostic$AIC
  print(paste0("AIC", GWR_AIC))
  GWR_AIC
}


z <- gwr_fitting(eq_wellb_no_spatial)
```

```{r}
for (eq in equations) {
  gwr_fitting(eq)
}
```

### Implement MGWR here.
